text: Rati points a knitting needle at the good chair. "Sit. Tea first, catastrophe after."
*Skull looks at the mud, then at your boots, then at you.*
*Skull watches you trip over the same root as last time, and files it away.*
*Skull sits among the standing stones like the only sensible one here.*
*Skull raises one ear, decides it isn't worth both, and lowers it.*
*Coach copies your stance, corrects it, and holds up six claws out of ten.*
Root: Left. Ring: Left worked in 1893. Leaf: There's a wasp. Hollow: I'm telling everyone what you just said.
Badger looks at your boots for a long, long time. "The mat. Use the mat."
Toad limps in wearing half a lilypad. "You should see the other pond."
Rati, before you say anything: yes, I wiped my feet, and no, I don't know who broke the {missing_need} situation.
Gust gestures a whole forecast at {target_actor_name}. I brace for weather, or gossip, or both.
I sit next to {target_actor_name} by the hearth and try to look like I also understood the joke.
I crouch beside {target_actor_name} on the trail and pretend the mud was part of my plan.
I stand with {target_actor_name} among the stones and resist the urge to fill the silence. Briefly.
I stay close to {target_actor_name}, who has clearly done this before and will not say how it went.
I step into the practice circle and ask the mirror to go easy on me. The mirror stretches.
I ask the old oak one question and immediately regret giving all four voices something to argue about.
